holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is up 6% on the day and 5.5% on the week at $139.55, a level that reflects a dramatic re-rating from the foundry/turnaround story rather than any dated catalyst in hand. With no confirmed earnings or ex-div on the calendar and only triple witching 79 days out as a macro marker, there's no event-driven edge here right now — the tape is doing the work and chasing strength without a catalyst is where small positions like this (8 shares) get shaken out.
INTC.bull: Momentum + turnaround narrative keeps bidding the stock until a dated catalyst forces a re-underwrite.
INTC.bear: No confirmed near-term catalyst means the +6% day can unwind just as fast on any headline reversal.

NVDA.read: No dated company catalyst on the tape for NVDA right now — the next earnings print isn't in the feed, and triple witching on 2026-09-18 is 79 days out and not name-specific. Price is holding just under $200 with a strong 1d (+2.57%) but flat over 5d, which reads as consolidation rather than a setup for action. Without a confirmed print date or a dividend event, there's no defined window to lean into either way.
NVDA.bull: Consolidating near $200 with AI capex cycle still intact; a confirmed earnings date would give a real add/trim window.
NVDA.bear: Stretched name with no near-term dated catalyst means gamma/positioning moves (like Sep triple witching) can drive price more than fundamentals.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down 11% over five sessions with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the provided calendar — so there's nothing near-term to lean into. The only scheduled item is triple witching ~79 days out, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one for a 15-share position. Without a catalyst anchor, the honest move is to let the tape stabilize and wait for the next earnings date to appear before committing to add-on-weakness.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event; no ORCL-specific catalyst on calendar)
ORCL.bull: OCI/AI backlog narrative intact; an 11% pullback into an eventual earnings print sets up a reload window if cloud growth reaccelerates.
ORCL.bear: Five-day drawdown with no catalyst to arrest it suggests estimate/multiple concern; without a dated event, weakness can extend on flows alone.

NOK.read: No dated company catalyst on the tape for NOK — no confirmed earnings date, no ex-div, and no position context (cost basis, P&L, recent price action) to lean on. The only scheduled event is triple witching on 2026-09-18, which is a market-structure event, not a NOK-specific driver. Until a Q3 print date lands or Nokia telegraphs a licensing/AI-networking update, there's no defined window to act on 174 shares.
NOK.bull: Ongoing AI/data-center networking narrative and licensing renewals can produce a re-rating catalyst if Q3 guide or a deal lands.
NOK.bear: Without a dated print, the position drifts with tape and options-expiry flows; any weakness ahead of triple witching is noise, not signal.

NBIS.read: NBIS is up 5.6% on the day but flat over five sessions, so today's pop looks like a single-day impulse rather than a fresh trend — and there's no company-specific dated catalyst on the board to anchor an add/trim window. With only triple witching 79 days out as a macro marker, the honest posture is to wait for an earnings date or a business update to print before committing to a stance. Until then, price action does the talking.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no confirmed NBIS catalyst on calendar)
NBIS.bull: AI infra demand narrative keeps bidding the name; today's 5.6% move shows dip-buyers still show up.
NBIS.bear: No dated catalyst to force a re-rate, and the 5d flatness says the impulse isn't yet a trend — vulnerable to a sentiment reversal.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position with no dated company catalyst on the board — no earnings or ex-div date provided — so there's no defined window to lean into. Today's -1.67% is noise inside a green 5-day (+1.15%), and the only thing on the calendar is triple witching ~79 days out, which is an index-flow event, not a KO-specific one. Without a confirmed print or ex-div date, this is a wait-for-a-catalyst-to-appear name.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific dated catalyst on file)
KO.bull: Defensive staple with dividend support; pullbacks in KO have historically been shallow and bought.
KO.bear: No near-term dated catalyst to force a re-rate, and staples can drift if rates back up or FX turns against them.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a fresh June 2026 IPO with no company-confirmed earnings or dividend dates on the tape yet — data vendors typically lag new listings by a quarter or two, so there's no dated catalyst to trade around. The only dated event in view is the Sep 18 triple witching, which is a market-structure event, not a SPCX-specific one, but freshly-listed names with thin float can whip around expiry as hedges reset. Best posture is to let the first post-IPO print (and any lockup/secondary calendar) surface before defining an add or trim window.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (market-wide; no SPCX-specific dated catalyst confirmed yet)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity value in a marquee IPO with limited public-market comps can sustain a bid until the first earnings print gives bears something concrete to short.
SPCX.bear: Post-IPO lockup expiry and the maiden earnings report are both undated landmines; thin float means triple-witching flows can amplify either direction.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
